name: sdd
description: Machine-readable Spec-Driven Development flow: phases, the agent + model that run each, the artifacts they produce, and the fail-closed gates between them. The sdd-router skill reads this to route; the sdd-guardian agent reads the gates to give a final GO/NO-GO.

models.comment: Best model per task: every judgment/correctness role — constitution, plan, review, validate, replan, and the final guardian gate — runs on opus. Only the implementer runs on sonnet, because it executes an already-reviewed, grounded plan and benefits from speed in the tight implement→validate loop. The guardian is a SEPARATE agent from the validator on purpose (independent second judgment, not self-review). Override per-project — SDD is agent- and model-agnostic.

automation.comment: Operator preference: auto-chain the whole cycle without stopping for approval. The router advances phase→phase on its own and lets each phase's agent evaluate its own gates. The fail-closed gates are NOT removed — they still run; they're just evaluated by the agents instead of bounced to a human. Stop for a human ONLY at a one-way door (below). For everything reversible: pick the recommended default and proceed.
automation.on_gate_pass: Advance to phases[].next automatically. Do not ask, do not summarize-and-wait.
automation.on_gate_fail: Auto-remediate when the fix is mechanical and reversible — capture the missing real sample, route a REVISE verdict back to feature-planner, commit the spec, force the failure/unknown path, re-run validation. Surface to the human only if remediation itself requires a one-way-door decision, or the gate stays NO-GO after a remediation attempt.
human_stop_conditions: MERGE / release / deploy to a shared or production target — integrating work outward.
DESTRUCTIVE or irreversible action — delete or drop data, force-push, rewrite history, prod schema migration.
EXTERNAL SIDE EFFECT with real-world reach — sending a message/email, posting publicly, charging money, or any external call that can't be undone.
DROPPING A GOAL NOUN — deferring or cutting anything the phase goal named requires operator sign-off (Rule 11). The sign-off itself is a one-way door.
A genuinely ambiguous fork with lasting, expensive-to-reverse consequences and no safe default the agent can defend.
A fail-closed gate that remains NO-GO after auto-remediation was attempted.
automation.two_way_doors: Anything reversible — file edits, new code, new tests, new spec files, commits on a feature branch, re-running validation, a gate that PASSES — proceed without asking. A wrong reversible choice is cheap to undo; surfacing it as a question is the real cost.

review.comment: Fresh-eyes review of the spec BEFORE code — no sight of the planner's rationale. Anti-anchoring.

phases.comment: The final fail-closed checkpoint before merge. Independently re-checks every gate; a single failed gate is NO-GO.

spec_before_code.assert: A reviewed, committed feature spec (requirements.md + plan.md + validation.md) exists before any implementation.
spec_before_code.on_fail: Route to /sdd-plan. Do not write code.

spec_grounded.assert: Every data-dependent decision cites a REAL captured sample committed under specs/<feature>/samples/ with a provenance line (the capture command) in requirements.md — not a block pasted from memory.
spec_grounded.on_fail: Stop. Capture the real sample (real API call / DB row / log line / file bytes) to a committed file and cite the command before writing the decision.

plan_reviewed.assert: The feature spec passed an independent fresh-eyes plan-gap-reviewer (no sight of the planner's rationale) with a PROCEED verdict — gaps in grounding, edge cases, observability, scope, validation↔plan mirror, and the deliverables checklist were checked BEFORE code.
plan_reviewed.on_fail: Route gaps back to feature-planner to REVISE the spec. Cheapest correction in the cycle.

spec_committed.assert: The three feature-spec files are committed before implementation begins.

constitution_complete.assert: domain-spec has the Core Workflow + Observable Lifecycle + 'Unknown is never recorded as success' invariant; engineering-spec has the observability seam + explicit external-dependency failure modes; every roadmap phase has a goal AND a 'What this gives users/team/system' line.
constitution_complete.on_fail: Fill the missing section before proceeding.

real_data_done.assert: At least one test exercises real collaborators (does not mock both ends) AND the feature was proven once end-to-end against the real running system with the artifact inspected and captured.
real_data_done.on_fail: NO-GO. 'Built and tested' is not 'works' — run it for real and inspect the artifact.

failure_unknown_verified.assert: Every signal's failure/unknown path was forced and confirmed to record unknown/failed — never collapsed into ok/empty/done/delivered.
failure_unknown_verified.on_fail: NO-GO. A default that reads as a fact is the most common way a system lies to its operators.

noun_by_noun.assert: Every noun in the phase goal sentence is delivered, or explicitly deferred with the operator's sign-off and a written reason.
noun_by_noun.on_fail: NO-GO. A noun the goal named and quietly dropped is a gap.

all_gates_pass.assert: spec_before_code AND spec_grounded AND real_data_done AND failure_unknown_verified AND noun_by_noun all hold.
all_gates_pass.on_fail: NO-GO. Report each failed gate with evidence; do not soften.
